originalTitle: 5 things I already love from the iOS 27 beta
summary: iOS 27 has only been out for a few hours, and I've been messing around with the developer beta on my iPhone 16 Pro. I was most interested in trying out the new Siri AI, but unfortunately, I'm still on Apple's waitlist for that. In the meantime, I've been poking around a bunch of features […]
content: iOS 27 has only been out for a few hours, and I’ve been messing around with the developer beta on my iPhone 16 Pro. I was most interested in trying out the new Siri AI, but unfortunately, I’m still on Apple’s waitlist for that. In the meantime, I’ve been poking around a bunch of features that aren’t about AI and found a lot that I’m happy with — even though this doesn’t seem to be as big of an update as previous years, the fit and finish throughout the operating system is already great to see. Here are a few neat things that have jumped out at me. The Liquid Glass opacity slider should have been there from the start I’ve always thought the Liquid Glass redesign, introduced with iOS 26 last year, was fine — not great, but not particularly bad (well, after Apple cleaned up some early issues, at least). But the new opacity slider in iOS 27 is awesome: it lets you make Liquid Glass elements like tab bars more clear or more frosty, meaning you can see objects under them in different degrees of opacity. Want to really see what’s under your search bar? Go glassy. Would you prefer to keep things more readable? Opt for more of a tint. (You can probably guess which I prefer.) [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9570.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9571.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9572.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] App icons got an upgrade Apple took another pass of many of its icon designs introduced with iOS 26, and overall, they seem much better to me. The changes are typically subtle — tweaks to colors or adding a little more glassy texture — but I think they make things look much more polished. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9583.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_0035.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9584.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_0036.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] Different volume settings From settings, you can now — finally — set independent volumes for ringtones, alarms and timers, and alerts and system sounds. In the Sounds & Haptics menu, just toggle off the switches that let you match alarms and timers as well as alerts and system sounds with your ringtone volume, and you can adjust the more granular volume settings from there. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9577.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9576.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] Extra large widgets I have to confess that I never use widgets, but I saw that extra large widgets were coming with iOS 27 this year and I had to see a few of them for myself. They’re seriously huge; they take up an entire screen of apps (except for the dock). But I could see them being an extremely useful way to look at an entire calendar or a long list of to-dos. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9574.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9573.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] You can make the time really small on your Lock Screen While editing the Lock Screen, you can now put the time next to the date at the very top of the screen. That opens up more real estate, which is nice if you want to see more of your wallpaper image, like a family photo or a cool piece of art, or if you just want a little less clutter on your screen. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9580.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9581.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9582.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] We’ll be digging in a lot more over the next few days, so keep an eye on The Verge. And if you just want to see a giant list of promising features coming to Apple’s next big updates, I’ve got you covered.
chineseTitle: iOS 27 Beta 中我已经喜欢的 5 件事
chineseSummary: iOS 27 才发布几个小时，我就一直在 iPhone 16 Pro 上摆弄开发者测试版。我最感兴趣的是尝试新的 Siri AI，但不幸的是，我仍在苹果的候补名单上。与此同时，我一直在研究一系列功能 [...]
chineseContent: iOS 27 才发布几个小时，我一直在 iPhone 16 Pro 上摆弄开发者测试版。我最感兴趣的是尝试新的 Siri AI，但不幸的是，我仍在苹果的候补名单上。与此同时，我一直在研究一些与人工智能无关的功能，并发现了很多令我满意的功能——尽管这似乎没有前几年那么大的更新，但整个操作系统的适配和完成已经很不错了。以下是我突然想到的一些巧妙的事情。液体玻璃不透明度滑块应该从一开始就存在我一直认为去年 iOS 26 中引入的液体玻璃重新设计还不错——不是很好，但也不是特别糟糕（嗯，至少在苹果解决了一些早期问题之后）。但 iOS 27 中新的不透明度滑块非常棒：它可以让您使选项卡栏等液态玻璃元素变得更清晰或更模糊，这意味着您可以以不同的不透明度看到它们下面的对象。想真正查看搜索栏下方有什么吗？变得玻璃。您想让内容更具可读性吗？选择更多的色调。 （你可能猜到我更喜欢哪个。） [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9570.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [图片： https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9571.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/IMG_9572.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all] 应用程序图标得到了升级 苹果采取了iOS 26 中引入的许多图标设计的又一个版本，总的来说，它们对我来说似乎要好得多。这些变化通常是微妙的——调整颜色或添加更多的玻璃质感——但我认为它们让东西看起来更加精致
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
